skill.name: video-extend
skill.description: Extend an existing video clip with RunComfy using Google Veo 3-1 extend-video endpoints. The skill helps choose the full or fast endpoint, shape a continuation prompt, and run the RunComfy CLI.
skill.summary: Extend existing video clips through RunComfy Veo 3-1.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, URL, filesystem, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill legitimately invokes an external CLI, sends user media prompts to RunComfy, and documents local token storage.

medium_findings.title: External CLI execution through RunComfy
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs agents to run the RunComfy CLI. This is expected for the skill, but it executes an external tool and should stay limited to runcomfy commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and security notes directly document runcomfy execution. The allowed tool boundary narrows the risk, so this is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: User video and prompts sent to external service
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The workflow sends a source video URL and continuation prompt to RunComfy. This is core functionality, but users must understand the privacy boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file states that the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API and references RunComfy network endpoints. No evidence shows unauthorized exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documented local token storage
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with mitigation. The skill documents that login stores a token in a hidden config path. It also says the token uses mode 0600 and should not be logged.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage path and permissions are explicitly documented. This is sensitive credential handling, but the stated permissions reduce practical risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are documentation and allowlisted endpoints
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious networking. The URLs point to RunComfy pages, skills documentation, example input, and stated allowlisted RunComfy endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are readable Markdown links or expected service endpoints. I found no encoded destinations, webhook URLs, or unrelated domains.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The referenced lines contain description text, prompting guidance, endpoint selection text, or exit code documentation. No cryptographic algorithm or system reconnaissance command appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows normal prose and an exit code table, not MD5, SHA1, host enumeration, or environment probing.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misclassified as Ruby shell execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for Ruby backtick execution. The repeated static hits are Markdown inline code spans and fenced shell examples, not executable Ruby source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only file is Markdown and the backticks format commands or endpoint names. No Ruby file or Ruby execution context exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: External command pattern: runcomfy run
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses runcomfy run to submit video extension jobs. Keep command execution scoped to the declared RunComfy CLI commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented and central to the skill. The examples do not show arbitrary shell command construction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network pattern: RunComfy API submission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends video URLs and prompts to RunComfy service endpoints. This requires user awareness and suitable media permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The text states that the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API and lists outbound endpoint boundaries.

content.value_statement: Short AI video clips often stop before the story beat is complete. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to extend a user-provided video through RunComfy.
actual_capabilities: Chooses the full or fast Veo 3-1 extend endpoint based on quality and cost needs.
Builds a RunComfy CLI workflow for a source video URL and continuation prompt.
Gives prompt guidance for camera movement, scene continuity, and one-beat extensions.
Explains how to chain extended clips into a longer narrative sequence.
Documents exit codes, troubleshooting links, and related RunComfy video skills.
Describes token handling, output limits, and outbound endpoint boundaries.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, account access, and valid authentication before use.
Sends video URLs and prompts to RunComfy, so private media needs review first.
Extends existing clips only; it does not create a first clip from nothing.
Long chains can accumulate identity drift and may need a fresh image-to-video anchor.

use_cases.title: Extend a finished shot
use_cases.description: Continue a short generated clip so the final action has enough time to land.

use_cases.title: Draft narrative beats
use_cases.description: Build a longer sequence by extending one clear story beat at a time.

use_cases.title: Test lower-cost variations
use_cases.description: Use the fast endpoint for drafts, then switch to the full endpoint for delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Simple continuation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one existing clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video. Keep the same subject, lighting, and camera style. The next action is: [describe one clear beat].

prompt_templates.title: Camera-controlled extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the clip with the camera [staying static or moving slowly]. The subject [does one action]. Preserve the original mood and framing.

prompt_templates.title: Cost-aware draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fast extend endpoint for a draft. Extend this clip with one beat: [action]. Keep background motion minimal.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot chain plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for several linked extensions
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan three sequential extends from this clip. Each extend should contain one beat, a camera anchor, and a continuity note.

output_examples.input: A user provides an eight-second product clip and asks for a longer reveal.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the full extend endpoint, writes a one-beat continuation prompt, and saves the extended clip to the chosen output folder.

output_examples.input: A user wants several story beats from one seed video while controlling cost.
output: The assistant recommends fast extends for draft passes.
It separates the scene into short beats with explicit camera anchors.
It suggests using the full endpoint after the sequence is approved.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether the skill can make a new video from a still image.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that this skill extends an existing video and points the user to the image-to-video workflow instead.

best_practices: Use one main action beat per extension to reduce drift and confusing motion.
State the camera behavior clearly, even when the camera should remain still.
Review media sensitivity before sending source URLs or prompts to RunComfy.
anti_patterns: Do not pack several actions into one continuation prompt.
Do not use private or licensed media without permission for external processing.
Do not ask the skill to install unknown shell scripts or run non-RunComfy commands.

faq.question: What does this skill extend?
faq.answer: It extends an existing video clip by asking RunComfy Veo 3-1 to generate the next segment.

faq.question: Does it create videos from scratch?
faq.answer: No. It needs a source video URL. Use a text-to-video or image-to-video skill for the first clip.

faq.question: When should I use the fast endpoint?
faq.answer: Use it for drafts, cost checks, and early sequence planning before final generation.

faq.question: How do I get longer than one extension?
faq.answer: Feed each output clip into the next extend request and keep each prompt to one beat.

faq.question: What data leaves my environment?
faq.answer: The source video URL and continuation prompt are sent to RunComfy for processing.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
